title: PolarQuant DOA Gate-B, rehabilitated & re-confirmed on held-out LOCATA
fixes: incoherent wideband MUSIC (correct per-bin steering, many bins)
Lloyd-Max fixed-rate reconstruction-optimal baseline (replaces per-coord uniform)

frozen_config.reference: dominant source from clean data (both arms vs same source)

heldout: eval/task2 DICIT recordings (13, multiple static loudspeakers), unused at seal time
interpretation: The consumer-relative flip TRANSFERS to real, unseen microphone-array DOA when tested fairly and powerfully: a genuinely reconstruction-optimal code (Lloyd-Max, better reconstruction 13/13) is downstream-worse than the angle-preserving PolarQuant on 11/13. Demonstrated on fresh cross-task held-out data (multi-source), config sealed pre-run.
caveats: eval/task2 is multi-source: absolute error uses a dominant-source reference fixed on the clean data, so it is lenient in absolute terms, but the relative flip (both arms vs the same reference) is fair.
one sealed config; the earlier GO-P-2026-032 single-bin/uniform miss remains on the record.
prereg primary bar was >=7/13; the committed harness checked >=8/13; the 11/13 result clears both.
bearing: GO-P-2026-031 (simulation) + GO-P-2026-033 (real held-out): the consumer-relative reconstruction-flip is demonstrated both in simulation and on real array recordings; GO-P-2026-032 is the honest interim miss that diagnosed the under-powering/baseline flaws.
